file_level.general_notes: Best practices score: 10/10 (0 FAIL, 0 WARN, 6 PASS)

rules.detail: File is syntactically valid JSON. No trailing commas, no comments, all keys and string values are properly double-quoted.

rules.rule_name: No hardcoded secrets, passwords, or API keys
rules.detail: No secrets, tokens, passwords, or API keys detected. The file contains only standard package metadata, dependency versions, and a build script.
package.json — npm convention applies. All keys (name, version, private, type, description, main, scripts, license, devDependencies) follow npm's standard camelCase convention with no mixing.
Maximum nesting depth is 3 (root → scripts/devDependencies → their values), well within the 5-level limit.

All author-defined keys are descriptive and unambiguous (name, version, description, main, build, license, etc.). Dependency names like 'tsx' are third-party package identifiers, not author-chosen config keys, so they are not penalized.
File uses consistent 2-space indentation throughout. Not minified. Properly line-broken with one key per line.
